content.problemStatement:
Chef is playing a game with two sequences of non-negative integers A1,A2,…,ANA_1, A_2, \ldots, A_NA1​,A2​,…,AN​ and B1,B2,…,BMB_1, B_2, \ldots, B_MB1​,B2​,…,BM​. He has an integer VVV, which is initially equal to 000. Chef will play for a number of turns he chooses (possibly zero); he stops playing when he gets bored. In each turn of the game, Chef must perform one of the following operations:


Choose an integer XXX from AAA and change VVV to V∨XV \lor XV∨X, i.e. the bitwise OR of VVV and XXX.
Choose an integer XXX from BBB and change VVV to V∧XV \land XV∧X, i.e. the bitwise AND of VVV and XXX.
Find the number of possible distinct values which VVV can have after the game ends.
constraints: A1,A2,…,ANA_1, A_2, \ldots, A_NA1​,A2​,…,AN​ are pairwise distinct
B1,B2,…,BMB_1, B_2, \ldots, B_MB1​,B2​,…,BM​ are pairwise distinct
the sum of NNN over all test cases does not exceed 2202^{20}220
the sum of MMM over all test cases does not exceed 2202^{20}220
content.inputFormat:
The first line of the input contains a single integer TTT denoting the number of test cases. The description of TTT test cases follows.
The first line of each test case contains two space-separated integers NNN and MMM.
The second line contains NNN space-separated integers A1,A2,…,ANA_1, A_2, \ldots, A_NA1​,A2​,…,AN​.
The third line contains MMM space-separated integers B1,B2,…,BMB_1, B_2, \ldots, B_MB1​,B2​,…,BM​.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, print a single line containing one integer ― the number of possible values of VVV after the game ends.
